choices.text: Cloudflare picks the nearest user based on geolocation.

Cloudflare announces the same IP from many PoPs simultaneously; BGP (the internet's routing protocol) automatically delivers packets to the topologically closest PoP. One IP, many physical locations, automatic shortest-path routing — no client or server configuration required.
DNS returns different IPs per region.
TCP rerouting happens at the kernel.

questions.explanation: Anycast is a BGP-layer routing pattern. The internet's routing fabric resolves the nearest PoP automatically. Cloudflare doesn't pick the user's PoP; the ISP's routing tables do, and the routes update dynamically as the network shifts.

questions.prompt: Your app has global users but origin in US-East. Cache hit rate is 30%. Latency on cache misses is bad for APAC users. Right move?

choices.text: Improve cache hit rate (most leverage), or move origin to multi-region with reads near users (heavier change but solves the structural problem), or shift compute to Workers/edge so requests don't need to hit origin at all. Anycast gets users to a nearby PoP fast — origin location is your problem from there.

questions.explanation: Anycast doesn't fix slow origins. Three levers: more caching (so origin trips are rare), multi-region origin (so origin trips are short), or move logic to the edge (Workers) so origin isn't needed. Pick by problem shape and cost.
